case.paper_title: The Spousal Bump: Do Cross-Ethnic Marriages Increase Political Support in Multiethnic Democracies?
case.focal_claim: priming the first lady’s ethnicity increases support for President Yayi among her coethnics.

original_datasets.name: Benin2012survey

summary_statistics.info: Rows: 1104; Columns: 67; Key dtypes include float64 (30), int64 (12), object (25). Key variables non-missing: passage (1104), BoniVote (782), FonGroup (1101).
summary_statistics.describe: BoniVote (0/1): n=782, min=0, Q1=0, median=0, Q3=1, max=1; passage categories: Nago=320, Bariba=317, Femme=156, Control=156, FonFemme=155.

original_datasets.notes: Dataset includes multiple treatment arms in 'passage' (5 levels) versus 3 reported in the paper; the reanalysis includes all 5. DV 'BoniVote' has missing values; analyses drop rows with missing DV.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without additional restriction.

analysis_path.path_description: Filter out missing DV; encode passage with Helmert contrasts across 5 levels; sum-code FonGroup; fit logistic regression of BoniVote on passage, FonGroup, and their interactions; test the C4 x FonGroup interaction.
preprocessing_steps: Restrict to passage in [Control, Femme, Nago, Bariba, FonFemme].
Order passage levels and construct Helmert contrasts (C1..C4).

key_choices.outcome_measure: BoniVote (binary voting intention for President Yayi).
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: passage (Helmert-coded C1..C4) with interaction by FonGroup (sum-coded).
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Drop rows with missing BoniVote or missing FonGroup.
key_choices.data_processing: Manual construction of Helmert contrasts for passage and sum contrast for FonGroup.
key_choices.inference_rule: p-value threshold at 0.05 for the key interaction term (C4 x FonGroup).

outcome.name: BoniVote
outcome.definition: Binary indicator of intention to vote for President Yayi.

main_predictor.name: passage_helmert_C4 x FonGroup_c
main_predictor.definition: Interaction between the fourth Helmert contrast of passage (FonFemme vs other conditions) and sum-coded FonGroup (-0.5/0.5).

analysis_code.notes: Implements the 5-level logistic regression with Helmert contrasts and the key C4 x FonGroup interaction; outputs /app/data/task2_results.json.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow the five-level passage model; do not drop treatment arms without justification; test the C4 x FonGroup interaction.

tasks.task_instruction: Restrict analysis to Benin-related survey experiment data.

analysis_path.path_description: Using the same Benin survey experiment, fit logistic regression with 5-level Helmert-coded passage and sum-coded FonGroup, including their interaction; evaluate C4 x FonGroup.
preprocessing_steps: Restrict to passage in [Control, Femme, Nago, Bariba, FonFemme].
Order passage levels and construct Helmert contrasts (C1..C4).

key_choices.outcome_measure: BoniVote (binary voting intention for President Yayi).
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: passage (Helmert-coded C1..C4) with interaction by FonGroup (sum-coded).
key_choices.sample_restriction: Benin survey experiment data; drop missing DV.
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Drop rows with missing BoniVote or missing FonGroup.
key_choices.data_processing: Manual construction of Helmert contrasts for passage and sum contrast for FonGroup.
key_choices.inference_rule: p-value threshold at 0.05 for the key interaction term (C4 x FonGroup).

outcome.name: BoniVote
outcome.definition: Binary indicator of intention to vote for President Yayi.

main_predictor.name: passage_helmert_C4 x FonGroup_c
main_predictor.definition: Interaction between the fourth Helmert contrast of passage (FonFemme vs other conditions) and sum-coded FonGroup (-0.5/0.5).

analysis_code.notes: Same model and output as Task1; saves results to /app/data/task2_results.json.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the five-level model with Helmert contrasts as specified; test the C4 x FonGroup interaction.

files.data/task1_benin_spousal_bump.py: Python translation (3-level variant retained for reference; not used as entry). Reads /app/data/Benin2012survey.csv, fits logistic regression with Helmert contrasts for 3-level passage and FonGroup interaction, writes /app/data/task1_results.json.
files.data/task2_benin_spousal_bump.py: Primary Python entry for both tasks. Reads /app/data/Benin2012survey.csv, constructs Helmert contrasts for 5-level passage, sum-codes FonGroup, fits logistic regression with interactions, writes /app/data/task2_results.json.

codebase.notes: Original R scripts exist but are not executed per run policy; Python rewrites ensure /app/data IO and implement the specified models. Both tasks can be executed via the task2_benin_spousal_bump.py entry.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original Benin2012survey.csv dataset and the Python entrypoint. Produce compact JSON outputs containing coefficients, standard errors, and p-values focusing on the C4 x FonGroup interaction.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Key interaction coefficient and p-value (int_C4_FG) from the logistic regression; secondary metrics include log-likelihood and model df.
